# The Silk Road

The Silk Road was a network of trade routes that connected China with Central Asia,
the Middle East and the Mediterranean for many centuries. Despite the name, it
carried far more than silk: spices, paper, precious metals, glassware and ideas all
moved along it. Few traders travelled its entire length; goods passed from hand to
hand across many intermediaries.

## Exchange beyond goods

The routes spread religions, technologies and diseases as well as merchandise.
Buddhism reached East Asia along these paths, papermaking spread westward, and the
same connections that carried wealth also carried plague. The Silk Road declined as
sea routes became cheaper and safer, but for a long time it was a main artery linking
distant civilisations.
